items.title: Texas' Miller gets first commit in top-50 Ogden
items.summary: Coach Sean Miller landed his first recruit since taking over as Texas coach, in small forward Bo Ogden, whose father, Chris, is GM at Texas.

items.title: Va. Tech plan to add $229M to budget approved
items.summary: The Virginia Tech board of visitors overwhelmingly approved a plan to add $229 million to its athletics budget over the next four years.

items.title: Okla. State lands No. 44 hoops recruit Montonati
items.summary: Senior Jalen Montonati, the No. 44 player in the 2026 SportsCenter NEXT 100 basketball rankings, announced his commitment to Oklahoma State.

items.title: Iowa State's Lipsey injures knee, out 4-6 weeks
items.summary: Iowa State starting point guard Tamin Lipsey will miss four to six weeks after injuring his knee at practice Tuesday, the school announced.

items.title: Loyola Ramblers chaplain Sister Jean announces retirement due to health concerns
items.summary: Jean Dolores Schmidt, who is 106, rose to prominence during Loyola Chicago's epic 2018 NCAA tournament Final Four run.

items.title: Virginia Tech proposes adding $229M for athletics
items.summary: Virginia Tech has proposed adding an extra $229 million to its athletics budget over the next four years to help position the Hokies to compete with the top programs in the ACC, a crucial moment with the hire of a new football coach looming

items.title: NCAA grants former G League player eligibility
items.summary: NCAA ruled that former G League Ignite player Thierry Darlan, 21, is eligible to play Division I basketball at Santa Clara, his agent told ESPN.

items.title: Pearl steps down after 11 seasons at Auburn
items.summary: Bruce Pearl has stepped down as the men's hoops coach at Auburn and will move into an ambassador's role in the athletic department as special assistant to the athletics director, the school announced Monday.

items.title: Hakeem Olajuwon's son, Aziz, picks Stanford
items.summary: Aziz Olajuwon, a top-40 recruit and the son of NBA Hall of Famer Hakeem Olajuwon, has committed to Stanford, he told ESPN on Saturday.
